search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae ankle pain telehealth guideline PDF
site:dha.gov.ae muscle sprains strains Issue 2 PDF
site:doh.gov.ae ankle injury guideline Abu Dhabi
site:nice.org.uk ankle foot fracture Ottawa recommendations
candidate_sources_rejected: DHA osteoarthritis guideline as a complete ankle source
rejection_reasons: Osteoarthritis does not cover this mixed acute ankle and foot presentation.
Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Only the exact NICE recommendation was used; unstated rule components were not inferred.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for sprain- or strain-compatible ankle injury and selected fracture-imaging decisions; not exact for plantar fasciitis, heel pain, every foot complaint, or children-specific care.
setting_applicability: Partial: DHA telehealth and UK emergency or orthopaedic guidance versus a broader ClinicNote documentation template.
UAE_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates, for sprain or strain contexts; non-DHA imaging details require UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 and current NICE NG38, last reviewed 2025-06-23, were opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: DHA Issue 2 and NICE NG38 remain the current official copies identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Reviewed to establish that the DHA document applies to sprain and strain presentations; no additional broad ankle or foot legacy items were mapped from scope alone.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines sprains and strains, common ankle, knee, wrist and muscle contexts, Dubai telehealth scope, injury mechanisms, athletic activity, overexertion, repetitive motion, and recurrence risks.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped ankle-injury mechanism, symptom, function, examination, and recurrence fields in sprain-compatible presentations.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports pain, swelling, bruising, restricted movement, weight-bearing difficulty, instability, loss of function, mechanism, timing, force, direction, pop or snap, aggravating conditions, and previous episodes.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped neurovascular, deformity, fever, hot-joint, movement, and urgent concern fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Identifies severe pain or swelling, discoloration, weakness, movement loss, numbness, coldness, deformity, persistent symptoms, fracture or dislocation concern, and clinician-selected radiography, ultrasound, or MRI.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-documented RICE, activity, analgesia, physiotherapy, return, and follow-up fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented RICE, activity protection, mobilization, analgesia, physical therapy, return to activity, one-week review, worsening review, and urgent escalation for severe, neurovascular, deformity, weight-bearing, fever, or hot-joint concerns.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports selected clinician-entered ankle or foot X-ray review without importing unstated Ottawa-rule criteria.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers non-complex fracture assessment and follow-up, repeated pain assessment, definitive X-ray reporting, Ottawa ankle and foot rules, and selected scaphoid imaging.

unresolved_source_gaps: Plantar fasciitis, heel spur, locking, open wound, anterior drawer, talar tilt, exact Ottawa-rule components, fixed 24-hour or 1–2-week timing, and broad foot-pain causes remain unsupported.
The combined sprain or plantar-fasciitis diagnosis label remains clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
